name: Vilar de Canes - Benassal - El Bovalar Loop
name_original: El Alto Maestrat — Circular desde Vilar de Canes por El Rivet
description: This moderate 20.0-mile mountain biking route in El Alto Maestrat explores the scenic El Rivet Municipal Nature Park near Vilar de Canes.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Vilar De Canes offers a diverse experience across a rugged and varied natural landscape in the Alto Maestrazgo region of Castellón. The area features prominent mountains like Mount Machorral and the Sierra de Foies, alongside deep barrancos and natural springs. Riders can expect trails winding through centennial oak and holm oak forests, as well as historic rural paths. The terrain provides challenging climbs and rewarding descents, with an average altitude of 668 meters above sea level. Best mountain bike trails around Vilar De Canes The most popular mountain bike trail is Vilar de Canes - Benassal - El Bovalar Loop, a 20.0 miles (32.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the varied landscapes between Vilar de Canes and Benassal. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Font de la Teulería – Benassal loop from Benassal, a difficult 34.1 miles (54.9 km) path. It features substantial elevation gain through the region's rugged mountainous terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Font de la Teulería loop from Benassal, a 19.0 miles (30.6 km) trail leading through forested areas and offering scenic views, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Mountain biking around Vilar De Canes is defined by rugged mountains, deep barrancos, and ancient forests. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from moderate excursions to more challenging rides with significant climbs. The routes in Vilar De Canes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 180 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Vilar De Canes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Vilar de Canes?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike routes available around Vilar de Canes, offering a diverse range of experiences across the region's rugged and varied natural landscape.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Vilar de Canes?
A: While Vilar de Canes is known for its rugged terrain, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available. The region's network also includes 20 moderate routes, which can be suitable for families or less experienced riders looking for a manageable challenge. These often traverse historic rural paths and less demanding sections of the landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Vilar de Canes?
A: You can expect a varied and rugged terrain. Trails wind through prominent mountains like Mount Machorral and the Sierra de Foies, deep barrancos (ravines), and ancient forests of centennial oak and holm oak. The area's average altitude of 668 meters above sea level means you'll encounter challenging climbs and rewarding descents, often with spectacular panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Vilar de Canes area?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931096054/vilar-de-canes-benassal-el-bovalar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilar de Canes - Benassal - El Bovalar Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 32.3 km circular trail. Another option is the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31444575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de la Teulería – Benassal loop from Benassal&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 54.9 km through mountainous terrain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints accessible by mountain bike in Vilar de Canes?
A: The region offers several points of interest. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3984447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Serratella&lt;/a&gt;, known for its demanding yet rewarding routes and excellent views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1104473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Castle of Ares del Maestre&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic vistas over the valleys and mountains of Els Ports and Alt Maestrat. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3004757" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rivet Municipal Nature Park&lt;/a&gt; provides a serene setting through ancient forests.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Vilar de Canes?
A: The region's varied landscape makes it enjoyable across different seasons. During January and February, the almond blossom transforms the area into a vibrant spectacle of color, offering a unique visual experience. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for riding, while shaded forest trails can offer respite during warmer summer months.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly mountain bike trails in Vilar de Canes?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails can vary, many natural paths in Vilar de Canes are generally accessible. It's always recommended to keep dogs under control, especially in areas with livestock or protected flora like the El Rivet Municipal Nature Park. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your dog, as natural springs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4583231" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font d'en Segures&lt;/a&gt; can be refreshing stops.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trailheads in Vilar de Canes?
A: Vilar de Canes is a rural area, and parking is generally available in or near the village center. For specific trailheads, you may find designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots, particularly near popular starting points for routes that venture into the surrounding natural areas. Always check local signage for any restrictions.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Vilar de Canes?
A: The mountain bike trails around Vilar de Canes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging climbs to scenic descents, and the opportunity to explore ancient forests and historical paths. The blend of cultivated and wild areas provides varied scenery, making each ride unique.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Vilar de Canes offers several challenging routes for experienced riders. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21957675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of La Serratella – La Sarratella loop from Albocàsser&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 46.0 km trail with significant elevation gain, providing a demanding yet rewarding experience through the region's rugged mountainous terrain. There are 11 difficult routes in total to explore.

Q: Can I access Vilar de Canes mountain bike trails using public transport?
A: Public transport options to Vilar de Canes itself might be limited due to its rural location in the Alto Maestrazgo region. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to larger towns in Castellón, from which you might need to arrange further transport. Many riders prefer to drive to the area to access the various trailheads more conveniently.

Q: Are there any natural springs or waterways along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area is characterized by several barrancos (ravines) and natural springs. You might encounter Barranco de En Seguer, Rambla Carbonera, and Barranco de la Frescó, along with the Molinell River. Natural springs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4583231" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font d'en Segures&lt;/a&gt; provide refreshing stops and contribute to the lushness of certain areas, especially within ancient forests.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through historic villages or offer cultural insights?
A: The region is rich in 'caminos de historia' (paths of history), suggesting old routes and trails that connect the rural past with the present. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33832270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benassal – Historic Center of Benassal loop from Benassal&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 32.4 km route that allows you to explore the historic center of Benassal, offering a glimpse into the local culture and architecture.
tips: Centennial oak and holm oak forest, currently protected as a micro-reserve of flora. Spectacular place to enjoy a walk, a picnic or just admire nature in its purest form. Highly recommended to make a stop.

There is a water source at the Molinell farmhouse, approx. 2 km in the direction of the road you can find the monumental Carrasca de Culla and the La Carrasca Restaurant. In the bed of the Molinell river you can stop to rest or have a picnic.

faqs.question: How challenging is the El Alto Maestrat — Circular desde Vilar de Canes por El Rivet mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it's generally accessible for all skill levels, it does require good physical fitness due to its 32.3-kilometer distance and 770 meters of elevation gain. You can expect a mix of manageable ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The trail takes you through diverse Mediterranean woodland, primarily featuring Portuguese and kermes oak trees, within the El Rivet Municipal Nature Park. You'll traverse tablelands and ravines, with rivers and watercourses like the Molinell River adding to the scenery. The path offers beautiful panoramic views of the Maestrazgo mountains.

faqs.question: What notable sights will I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: A major highlight is cycling through the El Rivet Municipal Nature Park, known for its splendid Valencian oak forests, including monumental and centuries-old trees. You'll also enjoy panoramic views of the Maestrazgo mountains and pass by the Molinell River.

faqs.question: What wildlife or plants can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The natural environment is rich in flora, including a micro-reserve with various plant species like orchids. You might spot wildlife such as forest bats, wild boars, and foxes, especially in the protected areas of El Rivet Municipal Nature Park.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Alt Maestrat region, where this trail is located, offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn. These seasons typically provide milder temperatures and lush scenery, making for a more enjoyable ride. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to mountain bike in El Rivet Municipal Nature Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking within the El Rivet Municipal Nature Park. However, always respect local regulations and stay on marked trails to protect the natural environment.

faqs.question: Is the El Alto Maestrat — Circular desde Vilar de Canes por El Rivet trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the region are dog-friendly, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through protected areas like El Rivet Municipal Nature Park, to protect local wildlife and ensure a safe experience for everyone.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Vilar de Canes. You should be able to find parking within the village, close to the trailhead, to begin your circular mountain bike adventure.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with trails such as Font de l'Ombria, El Camí IT6TR1 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, and Caminos del Señorío de Vilar de Canes, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead in Vilar de Canes?
faqs.answer: Vilar de Canes is a small village, and while specific facilities might be limited, you can often find local cafes or small eateries for refreshments. For accommodation, it's advisable to check local guesthouses or rural lodgings in advance.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and 32.3 km distance, bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate safety gear including a helmet. Sun protection is also recommended, especially during sunnier months.
